description: Breads, porridges, and grain-based staples found in any era.
foods: Bread
A crusty round loaf, still warm from the oven, cracking open with a sound like a quiet promise.
Porridge
Thick and plain and perfect, the kind of breakfast that sticks to your ribs until well past noon.
Flatbread
Thin and floppy, torn by hand and used to scoop up whatever else is on the plate.
Pastry
Flaky golden layers that shatter at the first bite and leave crumbs on everything within reach.
Biscuit
Dense and crumbly, split open and spread with butter before it even has time to cool.
Oatcake
A sturdy little cake of pressed oats, plain enough to go with anything and filling enough to travel on.
Cornbread
Warm and slightly sweet, golden-crusted on the outside and soft as a whisper within.
Rice Ball
Sticky rice pressed into a careful shape, holding a small surprise at its center.
Dumpling
A plump pocket of dough pinched shut around something savory, bobbing in broth like a small boat.
Rye Loaf
Dark and dense, sliced thin because a little goes a long way and it keeps for days.
